label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. The question is asking for the date one week ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. Let me break this down step by step. First, I need to determine what today's date is. If the flight is booked for tomorrow, July 29, 2002, then today must be the day before that. So today is July 28, 2002. That makes sense because tomorrow would be the 29th. Now, the problem asks for the date one week ago from today. A week has seven days, so if today is July 28, subtracting seven days would give me the date one week ago. Let me count backwards day by day to make sure I don't make a mistake. Starting from July 28, 2002: - One day before is July 27 - Two days before is July 26 - Three days before is July 25 - Four days before is July 24 - Five days before is July 23 - Six days before is July 22 - Seven days before is July 21 So, one week ago from July 28 is July 21, 2002. Let me check if there's a month change here. July has 31 days, so going back seven days from the 28th doesn't take us into June. July 21 is still within July. Therefore, the date should be 07/21/2002. Looking at the options: (A) 08/18/2002 – August, which is later in the year, so not correct (B) 10/21/2002 – October, way later, nope (C) 07/20/2002 – July 20th, but we needed 21st (D) 10/17/2002 – October again, no (E) 07/21/2002 – This matches our calculation (F) 11/21/2001 – November 2001, which is over a year ago, not correct Therefore, the correct answer should be option E, 07/21/2002. Let me just confirm once more by subtracting 7
